intro: Seddera glomerata, also known as the cluster seddera, is a perennial climbing plant native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Seddera glomerata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Seddera glomerata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
